pattern: Splitting on spaces isolates each token so only genuine dollar-prefixed numbers are discounted and reformatted to two decimals.
hints: Process the sentence word by word; a word qualifies only if it is a dollar sign followed entirely by digits.
For a qualifying word, strip the dollar sign, apply the percentage discount, and rebuild it with exactly two decimal places.

To avoid floating-point drift, multiply the integer price by (100 minus discount) to get hundredths of a dollar, then format the integer and fractional parts as dollars.cents with a leading dollar sign.

edge_inputs.description: two prices and a non-price dollar token
input: there are $1 $2 and 5$ candies in the shop

edge_inputs.description: full discount and several non-price tokens
zero discount leaves value but adds decimals
a lone dollar sign is not a price
digits with letters after the dollar are not a price
dollar sign not at the start is not a price

stress.description: many words, a mix of prices and decoys, random discount
